findings.explanation: The skill has no allowed-tools restriction. It only needs Read, Write, and Edit to manage correction files in ~/.claude/skill-corrections/. Without allowed-tools, it inherits full access including Bash and network tools, which exceeds its stated purpose. However, the skill contains zero instructions to use Bash, network, or any tool beyond file I/O, so the actual risk is low.

The skill writes to ~/.claude/ subdirectory. This is a dedicated subdirectory (skill-corrections/) and does NOT access ~/.claude/settings.json, ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, or any other sensitive Claude config. The storage location is reasonable for persistent correction data.

findings.content: When ANY skill runs, the executing agent should check ~/.claude/skill-corrections/
findings.explanation: The skill proposes that other skills should read its correction files during execution. This is an advisory cross-skill integration pattern, not an actual injection. It cannot force other skills to comply -- it merely documents the intended consumption model. No enforcement mechanism exists.
